name: Bank Of Labor
description_short: Majority labor-owned bank serving hardworking people since 1924 with flexible digital and in-person banking options.
description_long:
Bank of Labor was established in 1924 and has spent a century serving diverse communities with personalized financial services. As the nation's only majority labor-owned and labor-operated bank, the institution has built its business around supporting organized labor and working families. The bank positions itself as understanding the unique financial needs of workers and communities that traditional banks may overlook.

Bank of Labor offers multiple banking channels to serve modern consumers, including mobile app access, online banking, and 24/7 customer service. Their service model emphasizes accessibility and flexibility, allowing customers to interact with the bank through their preferred method—whether digital or traditional. The website indicates they provide a range of financial tools and products, though specific offerings like checking accounts, savings accounts, and loan products are referenced generally rather than detailed.

What distinguishes Bank of Labor is its unique ownership structure and labor-focused mission. Unlike mainstream commercial banks, this institution is majority-owned by labor unions and operated with a specific commitment to serving organized labor workers and their families. This positioning creates a differentiated value proposition centered on community uplift and economic opportunity for working-class households.

Based on available website information, Bank of Labor operates as a traditional community bank with modern digital capabilities. However, potential customers should note that specific product details, interest rates, fees, and service terms are not detailed on the homepage. Those considering banking with this institution would need to contact them directly or visit additional pages to understand specific account types, lending products, and competitive terms compared to other banking options. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: Majority labor-owned structure provides unique alignment with union workers and organized labor members
100 years of operating history and established community presence since 1924
Multiple banking channels including mobile app, online banking, and 24/7 customer service
Explicit commitment to making financial tools accessible to underserved communities
Labor-focused mission and understanding of working-class financial needs
Emphasis on personalized banking and customer choice in how they access services
cons: Homepage lacks specific details about products, rates, fees, and account types
Limited information about geographic service area or branch locations on website
No transparency on minimum deposit requirements, APY rates, or loan terms visible on homepage
Website content heavily focused on marketing messaging rather than product specifications

contact.address: 6301 Glenwood St, Third Floor, Overland Park, KS 66202

best_for: Union members and organized labor workers seeking alignment with labor-owned institutions
Customers who prioritize community-focused banking over large corporate institutions
Working families seeking personalized service and flexible banking access options
diagnosis: Bank of Labor is best suited for union members, organized labor workers, and working families who value labor-owned institutions and community-focused banking. The main caveat is that the website provides limited specific product information—interested consumers must contact the bank directly to understand account options, rates, fees, and how their offerings compare to mainstream banking alternatives.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 6301 Glenwood St, Third Floor, Overland Park, KS 66202
